discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Two articles on leave approval setup merged with Standard and Advanced PRD opportunity assessments. Articles cover basic configuration steps; PRDs add customization depth (standard company-wide vs advanced per-policy/department/office), custom roles, sequential/parallel approval, and ultimate approver role. All sources unified into single comprehensive leave approval workflow.

Two articles on role assignment and task-based role management consolidated. First article covers assigning roles and permissions across platform features; second covers role-specific task management capabilities. Both emphasize role-based access control as foundation for approval flows. Merged into single role management task that enables approval routing.

Grouped tickets addressing inflexible approval routing mechanisms, lack of OR logic/parallel paths, and insufficient criteria options (client, project fields). All prevent administrators from configuring context-aware approval scenarios.

content_clusters.topic: Approval Workflow Feature Gaps Across Modules
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Grouped tickets identifying missing approval workflows in business travel, payroll modifications, and commission processing. Each represents a distinct module lacking dedicated approval infrastructure.

Grouped tickets addressing setup-phase limitations: admin-dependent activation, lack of custom role support, and ineffective feature flag enforcement. All prevent self-service or flexible approval configuration.
Grouped tickets addressing incorrect notification routing (+1 level user) and failure to update approval status when participants change. Both indicate systemic issues in approval state management.
Single ticket addressing UI limitations in approval workflow representation (missing tab in leave salary approval).

product_context.title: Opp Assessment: Leaves Approval Flow (Standard)
product_context.why_built: Existing leave approval flow lacked customization, forcing companies to use workarounds (dummy accounts, manual off-platform tracking, changing reporting lines). Organizations needed definable approval steps, sequential approval enforcement, multiple approvers for redundancy when unavailable, and ultimate approver for urgent situations. Standard flow provides company-wide configuration with customizable steps and role-based approvers.
key_design_decisions: Single company-wide approval flow configuration (vs per-department/office variations in Advanced)
Multiple approvers per step enable parallel approval for redundancy
Sequential approval option enforces critical approvers first
Ultimate leave approver role can bypass entire chain for urgent situations
Depends on existing role management system for approver assignment

product_context.title: Opp Assessment: Leaves Approval Flow (Advanced)
product_context.why_built: Organizations needed different leave approval workflows based on leave policy type, department, or office. Standard approval flow was too rigid for complex business structures requiring custom roles (Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) as approvers instead of only standard Line Manager hierarchy. Advanced flow improves approval efficiency and matches real business practices.
key_design_decisions: Support for custom approval roles beyond standard Line Manager hierarchy
Different approval flows configurable per leave policy, department, or office
Custom roles auto-assign based on organizational structure
Backup/delegation feature for approver unavailability scenarios
Approval sequence configuration to enforce critical approvers first

product_context.title: Opportunity Assessment - Payroll Approval Flow
product_context.why_built: Current offline payroll approval process required preparing reports, sending to multiple stakeholders via email, and manually tracking approvals. This caused delays in payroll processing resulting in late employee payouts. Payroll approval flow within Bayzat eliminates offline process, enables multi-level approval hierarchies, provides breakdown comparison with previous cycle, and tracks approval status through hierarchy levels.
key_design_decisions: Admin defines approval hierarchy per transaction type
System presents breakdown comparison with previous cycle for informed decisions
Approver reviews data including pay items, new joinees, exits, salary updates
Rejection requires full cycle restart with amendments
Track approval status through hierarchy levels
Upon final approval, finalize transactions or close pay cycle

product_context.title: PRD - Payroll Approval Flow
product_context.why_built: Implement maker-checker controls for financial transactions. Allow organizations to configure multi-level approval hierarchies where payroll transactions must be reviewed and approved by designated approvers (HR managers, finance managers) before processing, aligning with organization DOA (Delegation of Authority) matrix requirements.
key_design_decisions: Maker-checker control pattern: transaction creator and approver are different roles
Multi-level approval hierarchies with role-based approvers
Transaction summary popup shows employees, total amount, approvers, license breakdown
Approver dashboard with summary and comparison data
Email notifications for pending approvals
Comments/remarks system for communication between submitter and approvers
If no approval flow configured, transactions process automatically

product_context.title: PRD - Decoupling Payroll from Expense approval flow (Reimbursement + Cards)
product_context.why_built: Expense approval flow (work reimbursement and corporate cards) was coupled to Payroll module, forcing clients to enable payroll even if they only needed expense management. This created confusing UI/UX for non-payroll users. Decoupling allows independent feature activation and removes payroll-related UI when payroll disabled.
key_design_decisions: Work expenses toggle moved to Manage Features (independent from Manage Payroll)
Employee Work Expenses header moved from Payroll section to Company Expenses section
Hide "Add to Payroll" tab when payroll disabled
Replace "Add to Payroll" CTA with "Mark as settled" for reimbursements
Corporate cards section unchanged regardless of payroll status
Employee profile shows Payroll tab when payroll enabled, Work Reimbursement tab when disabled

detected_integrations.description: Approval flows depend on role management system to assign roles to employees and select roles as approvers. Roles determine which approval chains employees are placed in and which approvers can act on requests.
Leave approval flow routes leave requests through configured approvers. Approved leaves flow to payroll for salary calculations. Leave request submission originates from employee self-service.

Payroll approval flow enforces maker-checker controls for payroll transactions. Transactions must be approved before processing through payroll processors (Lulu, SAB, Mudad). EOS (End of Service) transactions also follow approval flow.

Expense approval flow routes work reimbursement and corporate card requests through configured approvers. Can operate independently from payroll module. Approved expenses can be marked as settled or added to payroll.
Email notifications alert approvers when transactions/requests are pending approval. Notifications sent at appropriate approval steps. Reminders sent for pending approvals.

Approval routing based on assigned roles. Only Super Admin can configure approval flows. Approvers must have appropriate roles assigned. Role-based access controls limit actions available to each user.
Department, office, and reporting line hierarchy used to determine applicable approval flows. Custom roles (Department Manager, Office Manager) auto-assign based on organizational structure.
Approval actions logged for compliance and tracking. Approval status tracked through hierarchy levels. Comments and remarks recorded for communication history.
Work expense and business travel modules lack dedicated approval workflows. Approval routing must support client and project field criteria for expense categorization.
Approval workflows depend on role-based access control; system currently supports only predefined roles and lacks custom role configuration for approval hierarchies.
Feature flag configuration is ineffective at controlling access to approval flow setup screens for admin users.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Configure Advanced Leave Approval Flow by Policy, Department, or Office
steps: Navigate to Automations > Approval flows > Leaves tab
Create custom approval roles if needed (e.g., Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager)
Define separate approval flows for different leave policies
Optionally define separate approval flows per department or office
For each flow, add approval steps and select approvers (Line Manager levels, custom roles, or specific employees)
Set approval sequence to enforce critical approvers first
Configure backup/alternate approvers for unavailability scenarios
Save each approval flow configuration
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Advanced leave approval flows are configured with customization by leave policy, department, or office. Employees are routed to the correct approval workflow based on their policy, department, and office assignment. Custom roles enable approval chains matching real organizational hierarchy.
rules_and_constraints: Only Super Admin can configure approval flows
Custom roles (Department Manager, Office Manager) auto-assign based on organizational structure
Standard flow applies single company-wide configuration; Advanced flow varies by policy/department/office
Sequential approval enforces critical approvers to review first
Multiple approvers per step provide redundancy when someone unavailable
Line Manager levels can be selected as approvers in workflow
edge_cases: Approver unavailability (vacation, illness, extended leave) requires backup approver configuration
Urgent approval requests when primary approvers unavailable can use ultimate approver bypass
Customers previously used workarounds: changing reporting lines, creating dummy accounts, tracking offline

source_articles.title: How to set up Leave approval flow

what_to_do.task: Configure Payroll Transaction Approval Flow Hierarchy
what_to_do.persona: HR Admin | Super Admin | Finance Manager
steps: Navigate to Automations > Approval flows > Payroll Transaction Request tab
Click Add approval step to define first approval level
Select approval condition type: AND (multiple approvals required), OR (either approver), or Step (sequential)
Select approver roles (Payroll Table Manager, Transaction Processor 1/2/3, Expense Manager, Super Admin)
Configure multiple approval roles if using AND condition
Optionally add additional approval steps for multi-level hierarchy
Review approval hierarchy configuration
Save approval flow settings
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Payroll transaction approval hierarchy is configured with defined approval steps and condition types. All payroll transactions will route through configured approvers based on their roles. Maker-checker controls are enforced for financial transactions.
rules_and_constraints: Three approval flow condition types available: AND (requires all approvals), OR (requires either approval), Step (sequential approvals)
Only Super Admin can configure approval flows
If no approval flow configured, transactions process automatically
Transactions move to approval queue once workflow is set up
Approvers must be in approval hierarchy to act on transactions
Multi-level approvals must complete in sequence for Step conditions
Both payroll table and EOS (End of Service) transactions follow approval flow
Line Managers must be assigned permissions in Role Management before they can approve
If any approver in a step rejects, the entire request is rejected
Approval can be configured with multiple levels and roles
Multiple approvers per step enable parallel approval (any approver can approve)
Sequential approval enforces order of approvers
Ultimate leave approver can skip entire approval chain for urgent situations
Transactions require multiple approvals based on configured workflow
Approvers cannot modify submitted transactions
Rejected transactions cannot be resubmitted - must be amended in payroll table first
Transactions are organized by status: pending, approved, rejected
Rejection requires remarks for amendment guidance
Amounts from rejected transactions are returned to payroll table
Email notifications required for pending approvals
Comparison with previous cycle required for informed decisions
edge_cases: Approver unavailable or on leave - need backup approver handling
Circular approval dependencies must be avoided in configuration
Transaction edited after submission but before approval
Approver role removed mid-workflow
Multiple transactions pending for same approver
Rejection after partial multi-level approval
Approver attempting to approve their own transaction
If leave approval flow is not visible in Settings, contact customer success team
Approvers can be delegated if primary approver is unavailable
Multiple mandatory approvers can be added to same step for redundancy
Ensure each role is properly assigned to relevant employees to prevent approval routing issues
Transactions can be rejected even after initial approval
Rejected transactions cannot be moved back to pending status
Amounts from rejected transactions are returned to payroll table
Changes made after partial approval require full cycle restart
Concurrent approval requests for same transaction
Mobile vs web approval experience differences

source_articles.title: Set up Payroll Approval Flow hierarchy
How to set up Leave approval flow
Managing Transactions with Payroll Approval Flow

steps: Navigate to Automations > Approval flows > Work Expenses or Expenses section
Define approval hierarchy for expense requests
Set up sequence of approvers (typically Line Managers)
Configure Line Managers as approvers in Role Management if not already assigned
Optionally add multiple approval levels for complex hierarchies
Review approval flow configuration
Save approval flow settings
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Expense approval flow is configured with defined approver hierarchy. Work expense requests (reimbursements and corporate cards) will route through configured approvers. Expense feature operates independently from payroll when payroll is disabled.
rules_and_constraints: Only Super Admin can configure approval flows
Line Managers must have role assigned in Role Management before they can approve
If any approver in a step rejects, the entire request is immediately fully rejected
Expense approval flow can be configured independently from payroll
Work expenses can be enabled without requiring payroll module activation
edge_cases: Rejection by any approver in a step stops entire approval process
When payroll is disabled, "Add to Payroll" option is hidden from UI
Reimbursements show "Mark as settled" option when payroll disabled
Corporate cards section unchanged regardless of payroll status

what_to_do.task: Assign Roles and Manage Permissions for Approval Workflows
steps: Navigate to Settings > Organization > Role management
Click Assign Roles
Enter employee name(s) to assign roles to
Select appropriate role(s) from the list (can assign multiple roles to same employee)
Review role permissions by hovering over information icon to see specific actions
Confirm role assignments
Verify role assignments are complete before configuring approval flows
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employees are assigned appropriate roles with specific permissions. Roles are properly configured in the system so they can be selected as approvers in approval flow configurations. Role-based access control is enforced across platform features.
rules_and_constraints: Users can select multiple roles for a single employee
Different roles have specific predefined permissions
Roles are limited to specific actions within the platform
Some roles have overlapping permissions
Not all roles have full platform access
Specific actions are tied to specific roles
Roles can be granularly controlled
edge_cases: Some roles have overlapping permissions - review carefully
Employees without Task Manager policy need Customer Success Manager intervention
Employee Experience feature flag must be enabled for task creation by default employees
Role-based access limits certain task management actions
Custom roles can be created by Customer Success Manager if needed

source_articles.title: Assigning access permissions and role management

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Approval workflows cannot use dynamic criteria (client, project fields) or support OR logic and parallel approval paths. Administrators are constrained to predefined, sequential routing mechanisms that do not adapt to organizational context or manager availability.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Missing Approval Workflows in Key Modules
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Business travel requests, payroll table modifications, and commission processing lack dedicated approval workflows. These modules either have no approval mechanism or lack the flexibility to add remarks, warnings, and comprehensive status tracking during approval cycles.

Leave approval workflows and other approval features require administrative intervention to enable; users cannot self-service activate these features. Additionally, the system only supports predefined system roles (Super Admin, Leaves Manager) and does not allow custom role configurations. Feature flag controls are also ineffective at preventing unauthorized access to approval setup screens.

Approval email notifications are incorrectly routed to +1 level users instead of intended primary approvers. Additionally, when approval flow participants are modified (e.g., expense moved from approved to pending), the system fails to update approval status and synchronize visibility/action permissions, leaving approvers with stale information.
The leave salary approval workflow page lacks an additional tab for comprehensive workflow representation, limiting UI flexibility and user visibility into approval stages.

gaps_identified: Employee persona has zero tasks - employees submit requests but do not configure approval flows; this is expected as approval flow configuration is admin-only. However, employees should have visibility into approval status of their submitted requests (leave, expense, payroll impact) - this workflow is not covered in current batch.
Employee persona has minimal issue coverage (1 issue); approval flow limitations primarily affect HR Admin and People Manager workflows

uncovered_nav_entries.label: Role management
uncovered_nav_entries.purpose: Roles determine which approval chains employees are placed in

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
